mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 1.0pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge by more than 15% quarter-over-quarter, or Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which improves short-term corporate cash flow expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.0% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, which increases equity discount rates and compresses valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >50% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, or the negative correlation between inflation and returns breaks down during supply-driven inflation shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k while UNRATE declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that directly increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates fail to predict positive 20-day returns in >55% of quarters, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate manufacturing contraction and inventory drawdowns, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following the production drop, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below random chance (50%) in out-of-sample tests.
